concepts.title: Banach contraction principle
concepts.blurb: A contraction $f$ with Lipschitz constant $L<1$ on a complete metric space has a unique fixed point, reached by Picard iteration $x_{n+1}=f(x_n)$ with geometric convergence.

concepts.title: Brouwer fixed-point theorem
concepts.blurb: Every continuous self-map of a closed ball $D^n\to D^n$ has a fixed point; the proof goes through degree theory or the no-retraction lemma.

concepts.title: Schauder fixed-point theorem
concepts.blurb: A continuous self-map of a non-empty convex compact subset of a Banach space — or a compact operator on a closed bounded convex set — has a fixed point. The infinite-dimensional companion to Brouwer.

concepts.title: Lefschetz fixed-point theorem
concepts.blurb: If the Lefschetz number $\Lambda(f)=\sum (-1)^k\,\tr(f_*\colon H_k\to H_k)$ is non-zero, then $f$ has a fixed point. Brouwer is the special case of a contractible space.

concepts.title: Kakutani fixed-point theorem
concepts.blurb: An upper-hemicontinuous set-valued map $\varphi$ from a non-empty compact convex set to its non-empty closed convex subsets has a fixed point $x\in\varphi(x)$. The engine behind Nash's existence proof.

concepts.title: Applications across mathematics
concepts.blurb: Picard–Lindelöf for ODEs, the implicit function theorem, Nash equilibrium existence, and Markov-chain stationary distributions all reduce to one of these four fixed-point theorems.
